critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

Three deployment-readiness concerns:

1. SAFETY-NET DISTRIBUTION SHIFT: The recently acquired safety-net hospital has different patient characteristics than the training population. Deploying to this hospital without prospective validation introduces unquantified risk. The six-month timeline creates urgency for validation planning.

2. CAPACITY-DRIVEN THRESHOLD: The 80th percentile threshold was chosen based on call capacity, not cost-benefit optimization. The $45/$12,000 cost ratio allows computation of a break-even precision threshold. Whether the 80th percentile corresponds to sufficient precision to justify the intervention has not been verified.

3. HRRP NOT ANALYZED: HRRP penalty exposure depends on performance across specific diagnosis groups. Model performance stratified by HRRP-relevant diagnoses should be evaluated using the available 12-month holdout before launch.

Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.

empirical_test.measure: Safety-net prospective AUC-ROC; threshold precision vs. $45/$12,000 break-even; HRRP diagnosis-stratified holdout performance
empirical_test.success_criterion: Safety-net performance not substantially degraded; threshold cost-justified; HRRP diagnoses adequately identified
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Substantial safety-net performance degradation; threshold below cost-neutral; HRRP diagnoses systematically missed
